reasoning: Lilly King has held the women's 100m breaststroke long course world record (1:04.13) since July 2017 (over 8 years). The period from November to January is traditionally the "off-season" for peaking in long course meters, with most international swimming focus shifting to short course (25m) events and national training camps. Therefore, the chance of an athlete breaking a major long-course world record during this timeframe is exceptionally small. Probabilities are set at the maximum allowable confidence level for the nearing date, decreasing slightly for later dates to account for the larger time horizons.
